Description: Rocket uses declarative macros for route definition with #[get], #[post] etc and #[launch] for startup
Do: Use #[launch] for app setup; mount routes with routes![]; use Rocket.toml for configuration
Dont: Use manual server setup; define routes without macros

Description: Rocket manages state automatically via State<T> extractor with thread-safe access
Do: Register state with .manage(); extract with &State<T> in handlers
Dont: Use global statics; create resources per request

Description: Rocket request guards validate and extract data from requests before the handler runs
Do: Create FromRequest implementations for auth tokens, rate limiting; chain guards in handler args
Dont: Do validation inside handlers; repeat auth checks in every handler

Description: Implement Responder trait or use Result<T, E> where E: Responder for typed error responses
Do: Return Result<Json<T>, (Status, Json<ErrorBody>)>; use custom Responder implementations; use catchers for unhandled errors
Dont: Return Status alone without body; expose internal errors
Code Bad: // No catchers: Rocket returns HTML error pages by default\n// Not suitable for JSON APIs

Description: Rocket provides a Client for testing handlers without network overhead
Do: Use Client::tracked for stateful tests; test both success and error cases; check headers and status
Dont: Start a real HTTP server for tests
